mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment jumps exceed the 1.0pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, with negative outcomes exceeding 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates indicate robust aggregate economic activity, which immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 2.5% threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 2.5% yields positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered months, invalidating the tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, increasing wage growth expectations and driving positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during periods of high household debt service ratios signal immediate consumer distress, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines during high leverage periods fail to predict negative returns, with positive 5-day outcomes exceeding 55% of cases.
